title: Catch and Shoot on X-Out
game_context: 4-on-4 half-court shell. The strong-side wing drives baseline. The defense rotates: x4 tags the drive, your defender (x3) x-outs to the corner to take the corner shooter. The drive kicks the ball back cross-court to you on the weak wing — your defender just finished the x-out and is stopped in the corner with bad momentum.
possession_setup: You are the weak-side wing. The strong wing drove baseline. Your defender (x3) x-outed to cover the corner shooter. The strong wing finds you with the cross-court kickout. As the ball arrives, x3 is stopped in the corner with his weight going the wrong way — he has to reverse direction to closeout to you.
decision_moment: The ball arrives at your hands. x3 is in the corner with his hips and chest still facing the corner shooter; his back foot is planted to reverse direction, hands not yet up.
visible_cue: x3's chest is facing the corner, not you. His back foot is planted from the x-out. His hands are at his sides — the closeout has not started yet. You have a clean catch and a clean sightline at the rim.
prompt: The cross-court kickout arrives. x-out closeout is way too long.

choices.label: Catch and shoot it.
choices.feedback_text: Best read. The x-out closeout is the longest on the floor. Catch in your shooting pocket and rise immediately.

choices.label: Rip and drive — the closeout will be flying.
choices.feedback_text: Okay. If he flies past, the drive is real. But the shot is open right now — don't trade an open shot for a contested layup.
choices.partial_feedback_text: A drive can work if he flies. The shot was open at the catch — take it.

choices.label: Pass it back to the strong wing.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. The advantage was the cross-court kickout — passing back wastes it.

choices.label: Hold the ball and look for a swing.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. The shot is open at the catch. Holding lets x3 close the cushion.

best_read: Catch in shooting pocket, dip the ball, and rise. The x-out closeout starts from a stopped defender whose weight is going the wrong way — he cannot get hands up before the shot leaves. x4 is at the rim, x_slot can't leave the strong side — no second contest exists.
acceptable_reads: Rip and drive if x3's reversal flies past — produces a layup or kickout, but loses the open shot.
bad_reads: Pass back — wastes the cross-court advantage.
Hold and swing — x3 closes the cushion before the next pass.
common_miss_reason: Players see a defender running at them and put the ball on the floor by reflex. The cue is the closeout's starting momentum — when the defender just finished a sprint the other way, the shot is the cleanest read on the floor.
why_best_read_works: An x-out closeout is the worst-momentum closeout in basketball. The defender just decelerated to plant in the corner; now he has to reverse direction. He cannot get hands up before the catch lands in shooting pocket. The shot is open by definition; any extra dribble lets him recover.
decoder_teaching_point: When the closeout's momentum is wrong, the shot is open. Catch and rise — don't dribble.

feedback.correct: Good read. The x-out's momentum was wrong. You caught and shot before he could contest.
feedback.partial: A drive can work if the reversal flies past. The shot was open right now — take it.
feedback.wrong: Look at the defender's momentum. If he just sprinted to the corner and now has to come back, the shot is open.

self_review_checklist: Did I see x3 was committed to the corner when the pass left?
Did I catch in shooting pocket?
Did I rise on the catch, not after a dribble?
Did I follow through with a hand at the rim?

coach_validation.notes: Catch-and-shoot on x-out closeout is universally taught. Cluster fits beginner cap (3 pre / 3 post).

source_research_basis: Universally taught at every level; catch-and-shoot off an x-out is system-independent.
explanation_md: **Advantage or Reset — x-out variant.** When the defender just sprinted the other way to x-out, his momentum is wrong. Catch in shooting pocket and rise. The closeout can't beat a one-motion catch-and-shoot. Watch the defender's momentum, not his distance.

wrongDemos.caption: You drove. x3 stayed under control; x4 met the layup.
You passed it back. The advantage was wasted.
You held. x3 closed the cushion before the next pass.
